steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The first sentence establishes that Arabic-speaking Muslim states ruled the region for centuries; the second sentence describes Arabic words entering Spanish. The logical relationship is cause-and-effect: the centuries of Arabic rule is the cause, and the borrowing of Arabic words is the result.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: Signals that the second sentence illustrates the first. But Arabic words entering Spanish is not an example of Arabic-speaking rule — it is a consequence of it. ✗
  • Choice B — Instead,: Signals contrast or an alternative. Arabic words entering Spanish does not contrast with or replace the idea of Arabic-speaking rule; it follows from it. ✗
  • Choice C — Specifically,: Signals that the second sentence narrows or elaborates on the first. Arabic words entering Spanish is a downstream result, not a more precise restatement of Arabic-speaking rule. ✗
  • Choice D — Consequently,: Signals that the second sentence is the result of the first. Centuries of Arabic-speaking rule caused Arabic words to enter Spanish — a direct cause-and-effect fit. ✓
That is the correct answer.
